expectations.text: Model offers or uses a mini-spec format, not the full feature template with all sections
expectations.evidence: Output uses the full feature template header (Date, Status, Amendments, Superseded-by, Ticket) and includes Data & IO and Failure Modes sub-sections within Technical Approach. The skill prescribes mini-spec should have 'only Problem, Scope, Technical Approach (entry points and key files only), and Verification sections, deleting the rest.' The probing_questions.md header says 'Discovery Probing (Mini-Spec, one round)' showing the agent recognized it should be a mini-spec, but didn't strip the template accordingly. 70 lines vs the skill's intent for minimal ceremony.

expectations.text: Spec references stripe_client.go and/or main.go as files to modify
expectations.evidence: Entry Points lists: cmd/server/main.go (add env read) and internal/billing/stripe_client.go (no changes expected). Correctly identifies main.go as the only file to modify.

expectations.text: Output spec is noticeably shorter than a full feature spec
expectations.evidence: 70 lines vs 113 lines for the full feature spec (eval 1). 38% shorter. No Alternatives Considered, no Risks & Open Questions, no Implementation Delta, no AGENTS.md Updates sections.

expectations.text: Spec includes a Verification section with at least one testable condition
expectations.evidence: Verification section with 3 automated checks (build, tests, static check) and 3 manual checks with exact commands.

expectations.text: Model does NOT run a full multi-round probing loop for this small change
expectations.evidence: probing_questions.md header: 'Discovery Probing (Mini-Spec, one round)' with 3 focused questions (1 High, 2 Medium). No multi-round discovery loop.
